what:
OpenFn puntosolidario-rd adaptor. The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Punto Solidario.

Operations haven't been parsed for this adaptor yet — see the Sources tab for vendor API docs. Vendor homepage: https://puntosolidario.gob.do.
connect:
Gabinete de Coordinacion de Politicas Sociales (Dominican Republic) supports HTTP Basic auth for API access.

The credential schema asks for baseUrl, username (required), and password (required).
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Punto Solidario.

Triggers haven't been catalogued yet — drive the workflow from a cron schedule or an upstream webhook in the meantime. Gabinete de Coordinacion de Politicas Sociales (Dominican Republic) doesn't appear to expose webhooks, so plan on polling at a sensible interval.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Drive the workflow from a cron schedule or an upstream webhook trigger. Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Gabinete de Coordinacion de Politicas Sociales (Dominican Republic). Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Gabinete de Coordinacion de Politicas Sociales (Dominican Republic) jobs at volume:

- Gabinete de Coordinacion de Politicas Sociales (Dominican Republic) doesn't surface webhooks, so poll on the longest interval your SLA allows and use a since-cursor to avoid re-reading everything.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Dominican Republic social-services platform; no public API spec located.
best:
- Store Gabinete de Coordinacion de Politicas Sociales (Dominican Republic) credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-puntosolidario-rd to v1.0.0 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
